first_paragraph: **The Most Esteemed Family Order of Seri Utama**[[1]](#cite_note-1) ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Darjah Kerabat Seri Utama Yang Amat Dihormati*) is an [order](/wiki/Order_(decoration) "Order (decoration)") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It was established on 1 March 1954 by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) The order carries the [post-nominal letters](/wiki/Post-nominal_letters "Post-nominal letters") "DK II" as well as the [title](/wiki/Malay_styles_and_titles "Malay styles and titles") "Dato Seri Utama".

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

| Family Order of Seri Utama | |
| --- | --- |
| Awarded by the [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei") | |
| Type | [Order of chivalry](/wiki/Order_of_chivalry "Order of chivalry") |
| Established | 1954 |
| Country | [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei") |
| Eligibility | Members of the [Brunei royal family](/wiki/House_of_Bolkiah "House of Bolkiah"), foreign dignitaries, and other distinguished individuals |
| Status | Currently constituted |
| Sovereign | [Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah") |
| Precedence | |
| Next (higher) | [Family Order of Laila Utama](/wiki/Family_Order_of_Laila_Utama "Family Order of Laila Utama") |

|  | |

**The Most Esteemed Family Order of Seri Utama**[[1]](#cite_note-1) ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Darjah Kerabat Seri Utama Yang Amat Dihormati*) is an [order](/wiki/Order_(decoration) "Order (decoration)") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It was established on 1 March 1954 by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) The order carries the [post-nominal letters](/wiki/Post-nominal_letters "Post-nominal letters") "DK II" as well as the [title](/wiki/Malay_styles_and_titles "Malay styles and titles") "Dato Seri Utama".

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Family_Order_of_Seri_Utama&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Description")]

All of the emblems are made of [gold](/wiki/Gold "Gold") or [silver](/wiki/Silver "Silver") with [enamel](/wiki/Enamel_paint "Enamel paint") accents. The post-nominal letters DK II are associated with the order. "Dato Seri Utama" is a title that comes with the order.[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3)

## Recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Family_Order_of_Seri_Utama&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Recipients")]

Awards were given to members of the [Royal Family](/wiki/House_of_Bolkiah "House of Bolkiah"), princes from other countries, chiefs of state, and other deserving individuals.[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3)

### Ordinary recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Family_Order_of_Seri_Utama&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ordinary recipients")]

* 1962: [Prince Mohamed Bolkiah](/wiki/Prince_Mohamed_Bolkiah "Prince Mohamed Bolkiah")[[4]](#cite_note-:6-4)
* 1962: [Pengiran Muda Hashim](/wiki/Pengiran_Anak_Hashim "Pengiran Anak Hashim")[[4]](#cite_note-:6-4)
* 1964: [Yusof Husain](/wiki/Yusof_Husain "Yusof Husain")[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* 1967: [Pengiran Jaya](/wiki/Pengiran_Jaya "Pengiran Jaya")[[6]](#cite_note-:4-6)
* 1967: [Pengiran Anak Khamis](/wiki/Pengiran_Anak_Khamis "Pengiran Anak Khamis")**[[7]](#cite_note-7)**
* 1968: [Prince Sufri Bolkiah](/wiki/Prince_Sufri_Bolkiah "Prince Sufri Bolkiah")
* 1968: [Abdul Rahman Taha](/wiki/Abdul_Rahman_Taha "Abdul Rahman Taha")[[8]](#cite_note-:33-8)
* 1970: [Pengiran Anak Mohammad Yusof](/wiki/Pengiran_Anak_Mohammad_Yusof "Pengiran Anak Mohammad Yusof")*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: [Pengiran Mokhtar Puteh](/wiki/Pengiran_Mokhtar_Puteh "Pengiran Mokhtar Puteh")*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: [Pengiran Abdul Momin Othman](/wiki/Pengiran_Abdul_Momin_(born_1923) "Pengiran Abdul Momin (born 1923)")*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: [Pengiran Abu Bakar](/wiki/Pengiran_Abu_Bakar "Pengiran Abu Bakar")*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: [Pengiran Mokhtar Puteh](/wiki/Pengiran_Mokhtar_Puteh "Pengiran Mokhtar Puteh")*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: Pengiran Anak Chuchu*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: [Jamil Al-Sufri](/wiki/Jamil_Al-Sufri "Jamil Al-Sufri")*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: [Zain Serudin](/wiki/Zain_Serudin "Zain Serudin")*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: Abdul Rahman Taha*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: Mohammad Taha Hussein*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: Metali Yassin*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1970: Ismail Mat Serudin*[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9)*
* 1976: Rosnah Abdullah[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* *Unknown*: [Pengiran Abdul Momin Ismail](/wiki/Pengiran_Abdul_Momin_(born_1927) "Pengiran Abdul Momin (born 1927)")[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* *Unknown*: [Ibrahim Mohammad Jahfar](/wiki/Ibrahim_Mohammad_Jahfar "Ibrahim Mohammad Jahfar")[[12]](#cite_note-:1-12)
* *Unknown*: [Ismail Omar Abdul Aziz](/wiki/Ismail_Omar_Abdul_Aziz "Ismail Omar Abdul Aziz")[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* *Unknown*: [Princess Nor Ehsani](/wiki/Princess_Nor_Ehsani_of_Brunei "Princess Nor Ehsani of Brunei")[[14]](#cite_note-:13-14)

### Honorary recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Family_Order_of_Seri_Utama&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Honorary recipients")]

* 1958: Sultan [Abu Bakar of Pahang](/wiki/Abu_Bakar_of_Pahang "Abu Bakar of Pahang")[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* 1959: [Neil Lawson](/wiki/Neil_Lawson "Neil Lawson"): legal adviser to Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III[[16]](#cite_note-:14-16)
* 1963: [Dennis White](/wiki/Dennis_White_(colonial_administrator) "Dennis White (colonial administrator)"), [British High Commissioner to Brunei](/wiki/List_of_High_Commissioners_of_the_United_Kingdom_to_Brunei "List of High Commissioners of the United Kingdom to Brunei")[[17]](#cite_note-:3-17)
* 1963: [Angus Mackintosh](/wiki/Angus_MacKay_Mackintosh "Angus MacKay Mackintosh"), British High Commissioner to Brunei[[18]](#cite_note-:32-18)
